violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Employee moved can opener to 3-bay sink to washed, rinsed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2025

Diced tomatoes was observed held at (45-47) degrees Fahrenheit. Diced tomatoes were stored in unit for less than 4 hours, diced tomatoes were moved to a different cold station hold unit. Establishment shall not store tcs foods in unit until it is able to maintain an ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Establishment shall send inspector update once salad station adjusted/fix. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back ware-washing area, is not tight-fitting or in proper adjustment. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry for insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the several refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back ware-washing area, is not tight-fitting or in proper adjustment. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024

The following equipment were observed in poor repair: 1) A handle was lacking on the flip top of a prep unit 2) A handle was loose on the door of a small freezer unit 3) A leak was observed at the faucet of the three bay sink 4) The hand sink in the back area was loose (coming off the wall) New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the inside of refrigeration units have an accumulation of food debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/15/2024
The plumbing under the three bay sink was in poor repair (bucket catching leak under one of the bays). New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an adequate air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024
The door to the women's restroom is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024
The Flip top was observed not able to fully shut due to broken hinges. Per PIC parts are on order. New Violation. Correct By: 09/11/2023

Various foods were observed held at 44-53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food that was out of temperature for less than 4 hours was moved to colder refrigeration to rapidly cool. All other food was voluntarily disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2023

Establishment was using time for their pizza slices without permission. Establishment to email southern supervisor/senior with procedures regarding pizza slices. Establishment to cease using time for pizza slices until given permission. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2023
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2023

Deli meat was observed thawing on the counter in stagnant water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2023

Thermometers are lacking in multiple refrigeration units. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2023

The following equipment was observed in poor repair: The salad prep unit was observed at 47 degrees ambient. The grill prep unit was observed at 45 degrees ambient. The Flip top was observed not able to fully shut due to broken hinges. The walk-in was iced over an unable to recover temperature adequately. Gasket was falling down on sauce fridge. Refrigeration arrived at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2023
